title: German Navy to Deploy New MBDA and Rheinmetall Laser Weapon by 2029
summary: The Federal Office of Bundeswehr Equipment, Information Technology and In-Service Support (BAAINBw) signed a contract on July 9, 2026, with MBDA Deutschland and Rheinmetall Waffe. This agreement aims to develop and deploy a high-energy laser weapon system for the German Navy by 2029.

title: Chinese J-15 Fighter Seen Launching From A Carrier With Four Anti-Ship Missiles For First Time
summary: A Chinese J-15 fighter jet has been observed launching from a carrier, reportedly the catapult-equipped Fujian, while carrying four anti-ship missiles. This marks the first time such a configuration has been seen, indicating the J-15T's enhanced strike potential.

title: Zelenskyy taps European allies to build Freya, a cheaper Patriot-alternative to Russia’s ballistic missiles
summary: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy is engaging European allies to develop "Freya," a new, more affordable ballistic missile defense system. Ukraine plans to hold its first coalition meeting on this homegrown alternative to the Patriot system in France soon.

title: US Marines successfully test-fire new medium-range air defense system
summary: U.S. Marines on Guam successfully conducted a test-fire of their new Medium-Range Intercept Capability (MRIC) system. This test occurred during Exercise Valiant Shield, demonstrating the system's capabilities.

title: EU Signs Grant for MBDA-Led HYDIS Hypersonic Interceptor Project
summary: The European Commission and MBDA have signed agreements for the HYDIS project, launching a three-year concept phase for the AQUILA hypersonic missile interceptor. This marks a significant milestone for the development of a future European counter-hypersonic and anti-ballistic interceptor.

title: Nvidia’s biggest RAM supplier just had a trillion-dollar debut on Wall Street
summary: As the AI boom boosts demand for RAM, SK Hynix - one of the world's biggest suppliers of memory chips - launched on Wall Street Friday. The South Korean chipmaker opened at $170 per share and raised $26.5 billion, surpassing Alibaba's record as the largest debut of a foreign company, according to reports from The […]

title: A decade later, Pokémon Go finally made good on its original promise
summary: When Niantic dropped the first Pokémon Go trailer in 2015, it was hard to grasp how a bunch of players could work together to catch a pokémon like Mewtwo.
